title: Merry Stories and Funny Pictures
content:
When the children have been good,
    That is, be it understood,
    Good at meal-times, good at play,
    Good all night and good all day--

title: Sophie Spoilall
content:
I never saw a girl or boy
    So prone as Sophie to destroy
    Whate'er she laid her hands upon,
    Though tough as wood, or hard as stone;

title: Cruel Frederick
content:
Here is cruel Frederick, see!
    A horrid wicked boy was he;
    He caught the flies, poor little things,
    And then tore off their tiny win

title: The Story Of The Man That Went Out Shooting
content:
This is the man that shoots the hares;
    This is the coat he always wears:
    With game-bag, powder-horn, and gun
    He's going out to have

title: Slovenly Betsy
content:
Betsy would never wash herself
    When from her bed she rose,
    But just as quickly as she could
    She hurried on her clothes.
    To kee

title: What Happened To Lazy Charlotte
content:
"Here, Charlotte," said Mamma one day.
    "These stockings knit while I'm away,
    And should you fail, be sure you'll find
    Mamma is stri

title: The Story Of The Inky Boys
content:
As he had often done before,
    The woolly-headed Black-a-moor
    One nice fine summer's day went out
    To see the shops, and walk about;

title: Shock-headed Peter
The Dreadful Story Of Harriet And The Matches
content:
It almost makes me cry to tell
    What foolish Harriet befell.
    Mamma and Nurse went out one day
    And left her all alone at play.

title: The Story Of Fidgety Philip
content:
"Let me see if Philip can
    Be a little gentleman;
    Let me see if he is able
    To sit still for once at table":
    Thus Papa bade Phil

title: The Cry-Baby
content:
"Oh, why are you always so bitterly crying?
    You surely will make yourself blind.
    What reason on earth for such sobbing and sighing,

title: Envious Minnie
content:
Now Minnie was a pretty girl,
    Her hair so gracefully did curl;
    She had a slender figure, too,
    And rosy cheeks, and eyes of blue.

title: The Story Of Little Suck-A-Thumb
content:
One day Mamma said "Conrad dear,
    I must go out and leave you here.
    But mind now, Conrad, what I say,
    Don't suck your thumb while I'

title: The Story Of Romping Polly
content:
"I pray you now, my little child,"
    Thus once a kind old lady
    Spoke to her niece in accents mild,
    "Do try to be more steady.
    I

title: The Dreadful Story Of Pauline And The Matches
content:
Mamma and Nurse went out one day,
    And left Pauline alone at play;
    Around the room she gayly sprang,
    Clapp'd her hands, and danced,

title: The Story Of Johnny Head-In-Air
content:
As he trudged along to school,
    It was always Johnny's rule
    To be looking at the sky
    And the clouds that floated by;
    But what j

title: The Story Of Augustus Who Would Not Have Any Soup
content:
Augustus was a chubby lad;
    Fat ruddy cheeks Augustus had:
    And everybody saw with joy
    The plump and hearty, healthy boy.
    He ate

title: The Story Of A Dirty Child
content:
The little girls whom now you'll see
    Were sisters in one family;
    And both enjoyed an equal share
    Of a kind mother's anxious care.

title: The Story Of Flying Robert
content:
When the rain comes tumbling down
    In the country or the town,
    All good little girls and boys
    Stay at home and mind their toys.

title: The Little Glutton
content:
Oh! how this Mary loved to eat,--
    It was her chief delight;
    She would have something, sour or sweet,
    To munch from morn till night.

title: Phoebe Ann, The Proud Girl
content:
This Phoebe Ann was a very proud girl,
    Her nose had always an upward curl.


    She thought herself better than all others beside,
